evidence: LongIntHashMap is tightly implemented: power-of-two sizing, linear probing, correct threshold check before insert, proper rehash that resets size=0 and re-inserts — clean internal state management
PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate is a clear 5-step method with each step named by inline comment; branching is necessary and distinct
LogDiceCalculator.compute handles zero denominators explicitly and guards against negative log argument — precise edge-case handling without defensive sprawl
convertConlluToWplChunks in Main.java handles two state variables (inSentence, sentencesInChunk) with clear transitions and correct finally-close for the writer — bounded mutation

handleSemanticFieldExplore in ExplorationHandlers is ~120 lines combining parameter extraction (8 params), validation, grammar config lookup, pattern derivation, query execution, and 5 separate response section builds — mixes 3 distinct concerns in one method
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti is ~100 lines with the same mixed-concern pattern; the inner intersection logic (commonCollocates.retainAll) is embedded inline rather than extracted
handleSemanticField in ExplorationHandlers is ~70 lines with the same response-building verbosity

Response building across all handlers repeats the pattern: Map<String,Object> section = new HashMap<>(); section.put(...); ... response.put(sectionName, section) — no extraction helper for any repeated section

In handleSemanticFieldExplore, the simplePattern literal '[xpos="JJ.*"]' vs '[xpos="NN.*|VB.*"]' is determined by an inline ternary with no named constant — magic pattern strings embedded in handler logic

CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens uses pattern.indexOf(']', i) for bracket-end detection without tracking nesting depth — this works for flat grammar patterns but would fail silently on nested brackets; the limitation is not documented
BlackLabSnippetParser static pattern fields (LEMMA_ATTR, XPOS_ATTR, etc.) lack the private modifier and are package-accessible, exposing implementation details of a utility class
SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern is ~90 lines but has clear step comments (Step 1, Step 2, etc.) and bounded state; the length is the main concern, not internal clarity

strengths: LongIntHashMap implements a non-trivial data structure with correct invariants and no leaking internal state
convertConlluToWplChunks manages two interacting state variables with clean transitions and correct resource cleanup in finally
PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate has direct, step-by-step control flow with each step serving a single purpose
LogDiceCalculator provides a mathematically clean formula implementation with precise zero-value guards
low_level_elegance.issue_character: The dominant low-level problem is that every HTTP handler method mixes parameter parsing, input validation, query dispatch, and manual map-based response construction in a single 70–120 line method body, making each handler method a monolith of mixed concerns at the function level.
low_level_elegance.score_rationale: The utility classes (LongIntHashMap, LogDiceCalculator, CqlUtils, PatternSubstitution) show good function-level discipline. However the handler layer — which is where most of the application's lines live — consistently violates the extraction principle by combining unrelated operations in long methods, and repeats the manual HashMap response-building pattern without any extraction. At a global anchor, the codebase sits slightly below average because the most-visited code paths (handlers) have the lowest internal discipline.

issues.summary: API handler methods each mix parameter parsing, validation, query execution, and response building in 70-120 line monolithic methods
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: ~120 lines, performs parameter extraction (8 params), grammar config lookup, pattern derivation, executeSurfacePattern call, and builds 5 separate response sections (parameters, seedCollocs, nouns, coreCollocs, edges)
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: ~100 lines with the same structure; the intersection loop is embedded inline
handleSemanticField: ~70 lines of adjective-profile list building with nested category logic inline
issues.suggestion: Extract each concern into a named private method: parseExploreParams(), validateRelation(), buildSeedCollocatesSection(), buildEdgesSection(), etc. Each handler method then reads as an orchestration of ~6 lines. This also makes the response structure explicit without requiring typed DTOs.

issues.summary: CQL pattern strings used for collocate filtering are hardcoded inline in handler logic without named constants
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore line: String simplePattern = relationConfig.get().collocatePosGroup() == PosGroup.ADJ ? "[xpos=\"JJ.*\"]" : "[xpos=\"NN.*|VB.*\"]";
The same xpos pattern strings appear in SemanticFieldExplorer as private static final constants (ADJECTIVE_PATTERN, NOUN_PATTERN) but are not reused here
issues.suggestion: Use the existing SemanticFieldExplorer.ADJECTIVE_PATTERN and NOUN_PATTERN constants (make them package-accessible or move them to a shared constants class) instead of repeating the literal strings in the handler.

issues.summary: CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens uses indexOf(']') without depth tracking, silently failing on nested brackets
evidence: CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens: int end = pattern.indexOf(']', i); — no nesting depth counter, so a pattern containing nested brackets (e.g. character classes or future grammar extensions) would split incorrectly
The Javadoc does not document this limitation
issues.suggestion: Replace the indexOf(']') scan with a depth-counting loop (depth++/depth-- on '['/']'), then document in the Javadoc that quoted strings containing brackets are not supported. Alternatively, add a Javadoc note explicitly stating the no-nesting assumption so future editors are warned.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser static compiled Pattern fields lack private modifier, leaking implementation details
evidence: static final java.util.regex.Pattern LEMMA_ATTR = ...; — package-accessible, not private
Same for XPOS_ATTR, UPOS_ATTR, SENT_BOUND_LEFT, SENT_BOUND_RIGHT, XML_SENT_OPEN, XML_SENT_CLOSE, LEMMA_ATTR_ANY — 7 pattern constants all package-accessible
These patterns are implementation details of the parser and should not be part of the package API
issues.suggestion: Add private modifier to all static Pattern fields in BlackLabSnippetParser. They are not used by any other class in the package.

root_causes: Handler methods were built by incremental addition — parameter validation, then query, then response fields — without periodic extraction, resulting in long mixed-concern methods
Response-building verbosity was never addressed because the project lacks a typed serialization layer
likely_symptoms: handler_method_monolith and raw_map_response_building (from abstraction_fitness) are the same root issue manifesting at different levels
possible_false_positives: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern length — the method's step-comment structure makes it readable despite its length; extraction could make it worse if the steps are not independently reusable
